title: Strength A
subtitle: Lower body + core
coaching_note: Hip hinge on RDLs — push hips back, not down. If lower back flares on RDLs, swap to bird dogs immediately. Don't chase weight, chase form.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.form_cue: Drive through heels, squeeze glutes at top. Keep ribs down.
exercises.why: Activates glutes before loading them. Protects lower back.

exercises.name: Leg Swings (front-back)
exercises.form_cue: Hold wall for balance. Controlled swing, not a flail.
exercises.why: Loosens hip flexors and hamstrings before squats and RDLs.

exercises.name: Hip Circles
exercises.form_cue: Big slow circles. Feel the hip socket open up.
exercises.why: Hip mobility for squats and split squats.

phases.name: Lower Body
phases.coaching_note: Start at 8kg per side. Add 1kg when all reps feel controlled with good form.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.form_cue: Hold one DB at chest. Chest tall, knees track toes, sit into heels. Don't let lower back round at the bottom.
exercises.why: Builds quad and glute strength. Goblet position keeps torso upright — easier on lower back than back squat.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift (DB)
exercises.form_cue: Push hips back, not down. Soft knee bend. DBs stay close to legs. Feel the hamstring stretch. Stop when lower back starts to round — that's your end range.
exercises.why: Hamstring and posterior chain strength. Critical for running economy and knee health.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.form_cue: Back foot on dip bar or chair. Front foot far enough forward so knee doesn't cave over toes. Torso upright. Add DBs when bodyweight feels easy.
exercises.why: Single-leg strength to address right-side dominance and imbalance. Protects knees on long runs.

exercises.name: Ankle Weight Leg Raises
exercises.form_cue: Lying on back. Lower back stays flat on floor throughout. Slow and controlled — no swinging.
exercises.why: Hip flexor and lower abs strength. Helps running posture and protects the lower back.

phases.name: Core
phases.coaching_note: These are your lower back's insurance policy. Don't rush them.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.form_cue: Lower back pressed firmly into floor the whole time. Opposite arm and leg extend slow. If lower back lifts, shorten the range.
exercises.why: Deep core stability without spinal loading. Safe for the lower back flag.

exercises.name: Bird Dog
exercises.form_cue: On all fours. Don't let the hip drop or rotate. Extend opposite arm and leg until parallel to floor. Hold 2 seconds.
exercises.why: QL and erector stabilisation. Sub for RDLs if lower back flared during the session.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.form_cue: Forearms down. Body straight line — no sagging hips, no raised butt. Breathe.
exercises.why: Full anterior core endurance. Foundation for everything else.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.form_cue: Hip stacked, don't let it sag. Modified on knee if needed.
exercises.why: Lateral core strength — directly targets the left QL and iliac crest area.

progression_notes: Week 1-2: 8kg DBs. Add 1kg per side every 1-2 weeks when all reps are clean. If lower back is sore, drop RDLs and add an extra set of bird dogs.
